text:
Assists from just one competition alone.
Salah's goal contributions record for 24:25 was just two away from tying them, including the other competitions.
Now, Salah had 57 goal contributions across the whole season.
However, problem is, once his form fizzled out, so did Liverpool, as you saw from their early exit in the Japanese league.
But it's figures like these that make it so hard for me to believe people when they say Laminal was actually robbed.
Like you want to talk about a real robbery, look.
Look at his teammate Hafinha.
Hafinha had 56 goal contributions across all competitions.
He tied Cristiano Ronaldo's Champions league record of 21 goal contributions in a single season and didn't receive a number one vote from any journalist.
Did you catch that?
Up to this point in the video I've been basing the winner of the Ballon d' Ors not off my criteria, but.
But the media's.
Because if you didn't know already, the Ballon d' or is entirely decided by international journalists, which in my opinion already a bad idea to get shit like this every year.
But it also means that this is an award based entirely on media perception.
A couple years ago there was a lot of talk about how football after the post Messi Ronaldo era was kinda boring.
It was too systematic, too calculated.
It almost kicked out the brilliance of the individual player themselves.
So when Lamine Yamal popped up on the scene and was making defenses defenders look silly, everyone saw him as this savior of football.
Especially the media who were getting tired of writing about the newest up and coming Magic's form of the Gagan penis tactic or whatever.
Now compare this to Hafinha.
The Brazilian grew up in a rough neighborhood where there were times when he and his family had to beg for food.
This was a guy who lost many friends to the lure of false hope.
Money from crime and drugs brought to people in desperation and he went from playing in some of the toughest environments you could think of to smashing records held by gods this sport.
But was he the savior of football?
No.
Back in the line buddy.
Let's go back to Dembele for a second.
Had PSG lost that final, I wholeheartedly believe that Lamin would have been in the pole position to win this thing.
Because basing this off what the media was saying, while Dembele's goal contributions significantly outweighed the means, total French success doesn't have the same pedigree as Spanish success.
And most importantly, Lamin was seen as the more impactful player on the pitch.
But the thing is, that's not true at all.
Because if you're an avid PSG fan or hate watcher like myself, you saw Dembele all over the pitch, and in doing so, he unlocked so much space for his teammates.
Not only was he scoring goals like an absolute, but he was also the reason why Parisanjma opened up so much, why they were playing so incredibly fluid.
But that doesn't matter because those other impactful highlights aren't as flashy as what Lamid Yamal is doing.
And also at the same time, it doesn't fit Dembele's narrative, because Dembele's narrative was the fact that he was finally fulfilling that career mode as potential we all expected from it when he was younger.
Meanwhile, you have Lamin's highlights who have been more impactful simply because they're flashy and they're also decorations for that savior of football title.
Enough about Lamin.
Let's look at how the media has affected the rest of the rankings this year.
At a time when it feels like the striker is becoming more and more rare.
Victor Gyokkur exploded onto the scene.
39 goals in the Portuguese league and another six in the Champions League put this guy 15th in the rankings.
That ranking was higher than 21st place.
Sergu Girassi, who had a better goal per 90 record in the Champions League.
But here's the thing.
Do you see Serhu doing those mass celebrations or having those aura edits of him?
Exactly.
Erling Holland had to put Manchester City's attack on his back because everyone else was either underwhelming or washed.
And also Pep was divorced.
22 goals in the the Premier League, 8 goals in the Champions League.
But because expectations are greater for him, his lower figures and missed shots were put under a microscope more than anyone else.
And in the end he placed 26.
Desiree due is another kid in the footballing world we can see as the savior of football in his individual brilliance.
He's absolutely saucy, but no doubt, but his performances versus Liverpool in the second leg and enter in the final very much skyrocket his notoriety because I'm sorry, I'm not about to be told that this guy had a better overall season than Joao Neves and even Scott McTominay.
Scott McTominay was essential to Napoli, Scudetto run and also one Serie A MVP.
But because Serie A doesn't get any international attention really, he's placed below the likes of Victor Jokers and Cole Palmer.
This is the biggest case of recency bias right here because Cole Palmer throughout the second half of the season had only three goals and two assists in the Premier League.
But because he starred in the Club World cup and let Chelsea pass PSG in a matter of like 30 minutes, he's ranked eighth.
That is 18 spots higher than last year when he had a better overall season.
Dude, he's higher than Chi Chi Donaruma.
Let's be serious for a second.
I think especially now that we're long past the Messi Ronaldo era, I think people are now really starting to see how this award is.
Why don't we just slap the biggest possible cannon on these things so that anything you're even close to hitting will just vaporize.
Why not stick extra auto cannons and missiles on them too to really make it a one stop shop for all your killing needs.
Target overkill the like and subscribe button and let's talk about it.
This is the M182 SEP V3 Abrams tank.
It's the most advanced.
